interpretation: Keeps all validated pairs and increases the cost of missing rare duplicate pairs. This is the preferred first strategy when positives are scarce.
Down-weights easy examples and concentrates gradient on hard or misclassified pairs. It is useful when the model otherwise learns only the dominant negative class.
Keeps non-duplicate pairs that look structurally similar to positives. This tests whether the model can separate difficult near-matches instead of only easy negatives.
